USA and Canada

Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship for Asylum Seekers and Refugees in the USA and Canada

Published 19 Jun 20266 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 19 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarships for Asylum Seekers and Refugees

If you are an asylum seeker or refugee looking to pursue your Master's or PhD studies in the USA or Canada, the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship is a great opportunity for you. The Fulbright Scholarship covers full tuition fees, living expenses, and other benefits, making it a prestigious and highly sought-after award. As an asylum seeker or refugee, you can apply for this scholarship to advance your education and build a better future for yourself.

Who Qualifies

To be eligible for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship, you must fall under one of the following visa categories:

* Asylum status (AS)

* Refugee status (RS)

* Temporary Protected Status (TPS)

* Special Immigrant Visa (SIV)

* Deferred Enforced Departure (DED)

* Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA)

* Withholding of Removal (WOR)

* Convention Against Torture (CAT)

Available Scholarships

The following scholarships are available for asylum seekers and refugees pursuing Master's and PhD studies in the USA and Canada:

Fulbright Foreign Student Program

The Fulbright Foreign Student Program provides full funding for international students, including asylum seekers and refugees, to pursue their Master's or PhD studies in the USA. The coverage amount is up to $50,000 per year, and the eligibility criteria include a bachelor's degree, English proficiency, and a strong academic record. The deadline for application is October 15th every year, and the official website is https://fulbright.amideast.org/.

Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships

The Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships provide $50,000 per year for three years to international students, including asylum seekers and refugees, pursuing their PhD studies in Canada. The eligibility criteria include a master's degree, English or French proficiency, and a strong academic record. The deadline for application is November 1st every year, and the official website is https://www.cihr-irsc.gc.ca/.

Trudeau Scholarships

The Trudeau Scholarships provide up to $40,000 per year for three years to international students, including asylum seekers and refugees, pursuing their PhD studies in Canada. The eligibility criteria include a master's degree, English or French proficiency, and a strong academic record. The deadline for application is December 15th every year, and the official website is https://www.trudeaufoundation.ca/.

DAAD Scholarship for Refugees

The DAAD Scholarship for Refugees provides up to €1,200 per month for international students, including asylum seekers and refugees, pursuing their Master's or PhD studies in the USA or Canada. The eligibility criteria include a bachelor's or master's degree, English or German proficiency, and a strong academic record. The deadline for application is January 15th every year, and the official website is https://www.daad.de/.

Ford Foundation Fellowship Program

The Ford Foundation Fellowship Program provides up to $50,000 per year for international students, including asylum seekers and refugees, pursuing their PhD studies in the USA. The eligibility criteria include a master's degree, English proficiency, and a strong academic record. The deadline for application is December 15th every year, and the official website is https://sites.nationalacademies.org/.

Application Steps

To apply for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship, follow these steps:

  • Review the eligibility criteria and ensure you meet the requirements.

  • Choose the scholarship you want to apply for and read the application instructions carefully.

  • Prepare your application documents, including your resume, transcripts, and letters of recommendation.

  • Submit your application online through the official website.

  • Wait for the application to be reviewed and processed.

  • If selected, you will be invited for an interview or further assessment.

  • If successful, you will receive the scholarship award letter and instructions on how to claim your award.

  • Use the scholarship to pursue your Master's or PhD studies in the USA or Canada.

Documents Checklist

To apply for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship, you will need to provide the following documents:

* Passport or travel document

* Asylum or refugee status document

* Birth certificate

* Marriage certificate (if applicable)

* Divorce or separation certificate (if applicable)

* Death certificate (if applicable)

* Academic transcripts

* Diplomas or degree certificates

* Letters of recommendation

* English or French proficiency test results

* Resume or CV

Frequently Asked Questions

  • What is the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship?

The Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship is a prestigious award that provides full funding for international students, including asylum seekers and refugees, to pursue their Master's or PhD studies in the USA or Canada. The scholarship covers full tuition fees, living expenses, and other benefits.

  • Can I apply for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship if I am an asylum seeker?

Yes, you can apply for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship if you are an asylum seeker. You must meet the eligibility criteria, including having a bachelor's or master's degree, English proficiency, and a strong academic record.

  • What are the eligibility criteria for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship?

The eligibility criteria for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship include having a bachelor's or master's degree, English or French proficiency, and a strong academic record. You must also fall under one of the eligible visa categories, including asylum status, refugee status, or temporary protected status.

  • How do I apply for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship?

To apply for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship, you must submit your application online through the official website. You will need to provide your application documents, including your resume, transcripts, and letters of recommendation.

  • What is the deadline for applying for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship?

The deadline for applying for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship varies depending on the scholarship you are applying for. You must check the official website for the specific deadline and ensure you submit your application on time.

  • Can I apply for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship if I am a refugee?

Yes, you can apply for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship if you are a refugee. You must meet the eligibility criteria, including having a bachelor's or master's degree, English proficiency, and a strong academic record.

  • How long does it take to process the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship application?

The processing time for the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship application varies depending on the scholarship you are applying for. You must check the official website for the specific processing time and ensure you plan accordingly.

  • Can I use the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship to pursue my studies in any field?

The Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship can be used to pursue studies in any field, including arts, humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and engineering. However, some scholarships may have specific field requirements, so you must check the eligibility criteria carefully.

External Resources

For more information on the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship, you can visit the official website of the US Department of State or the Canadian Government.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the Fully Funded Fulbright Scholarship is a great opportunity for asylum seekers and refugees to pursue their Master's or PhD studies in the USA or Canada. To learn more about scholarship opportunities and how to apply, visit the MigrantScholar blog.
